title: Whipping Cream and Fermented Dairy
content:
WHIPPING CREAM

Cream must be at least 30% fat (light whipping: 30-36%; heavy: 36-40%). Must be at least one day old; chill bowl and beaters at least 2 hours. Beat until falls in soft peaks with gloss (stiff but not dry). Doubles in volume.

Whipped Cream Substitutes:
I. Allow light cream to stand 48 hours; skim and whip
II. Soak 1-1¼ teaspoons gelatin in 2 tablespoons cold liquid; dissolve in ½ cup scalded light cream; add 1 cup light cream and 1 tablespoon confectioners' sugar; chill 4-6 hours; beat 5-7 minutes
III. Evaporated milk (chilled 12 hours) whips to 3× volume with 3 tablespoons lemon juice per 13-ounce can

WHIPPED CREAM STABILIZERS

Add confectioners' sugar (cornstarch acts as stabilizer) or small amount of gelatin if holding 24+ hours.

SOUR & FERMENTED DAIRY

Buttermilk: Originally residue from butter-making; now cultured skim milk. More quickly digested than skim milk due to fine curd. Substitution for sour milk in recipes.

Sour/Cultured Cream: 20%+ fat cream cultured with bacteria. Add at end of cooking over low heat; stir gently to avoid curdling. Does not freeze well.

Yogurt: Milk fermented with Lactobacillus bulgaricus. Heat milk to 180°F, cool to 105-110°F, add culture (2-3 tablespoons yogurt or package starter), maintain at 105-110°F 3-4 hours until custardy. Reserve 2-3 tablespoons for next batch. Keeps 6-7 days refrigerated.

Creme Fraiche: Raw 30% cream allowed to mature until nutty-flavored. Tolerates higher cooking temperatures than sour cream before curdling.

Substitution: 1 cup whipping cream + 1 teaspoon cultured buttermilk; heat to 85°F; hold at 60-85°F until thickened.

Substitutions:
- Sweet to sour milk: 1 tablespoon lemon juice or vinegar in measuring cup; fill with milk; stand 5 minutes
- Sour milk in recipe: For each 1 teaspoon baking powder, use ½ teaspoon soda + ½ cup sour milk (or ½ tablespoon vinegar + sweet milk to make ½ cup)
- Light cream: ⅞ cup milk + 3 tablespoons butter
- Whipping cream: ¾ cup milk + ⅓ cup butter (will not whip)
